rulings.Case Title: LYDIA BACA VS. MALINKA TACUMA MOYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Moton To Set Aside Fraudulent Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT MALINKA MOYE WADE, AN INDIVIDUAL Moton To Set Aside Fraudulent Default Judgment. Recusal Of Judge Ronald Quidachay Ca Franchise Tax Board & Irs To Presecute Fine Plaintiffs, Debrief, Collect Full Proceeds Of Local, State & Federal Taxes Scheduling Administrative Proceeds By Us Tax Court Cases 20471-W & 19685-24. Jp Morgan Chase Wells Fargo Bank & Bank Of America Filing Charges is DENIED. The Court has already denied prior motions by Defendant to set aside the default judgment, most recently on October 23, 2024 and December 4, 2024. Defendant's new motion does not state grounds for setting aside default judgment, and there is no Proof of Service for this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Set Aside And Vacate The Court'S Orders And Judgment Issued On April 1, 2025
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS Notice Of Motion; Motion To Set Aside And Vacate The Court'S Orders And Judgment Issued On April 1, 2025 is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show new facts or circumstances suggesting the Court's prior orders should be vacated and failed to show mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Plaintiff simply repeats arguments raised in the prior motions, which the Court rejected. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ISHIDEN 6, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. THE BULKLEY FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP; A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT REGENT LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16 is DENIED. Defendant failed to establish that the challenged claim "arises from activity protected by section 425.16." (Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 376, 395; Civ. Code sec. 425.16, subd. (b)(1).) Plaintiffs' action, filed prior to Defendant's partition action, arose from an underlying dispute regarding Defendant's fiduciary duty and request for accounting. As such, Defendant failed to meet its initial burden. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NEW YORK BRANCH, A NEW YORK VS. PINE & POWELL PARTNERS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL ROSENFELD, PINE & POWELL PARTNERS, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege a viable cause of action. Specific performance is a remedy, not a standalone cause of action. Defendant did not waive its right to demur. Plaintiff's cited authority has no clear indication that the Complaints in those cases named specific performance as a cause of action. And the fact that this Court has appointed a receiver as a stipulated, provisional remedy does not constitute a holding that specific performance is a viable, standalone cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JEFFREY CROCIANI VS. RYAN CAHILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 10. DEFENDANT RYAN CAHILL, MILES ATKINSON MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED, 5 days to serve and file a response. The proofs of service of summons state that copies of the summons and complaint were posted and mailed to each defendant by certified mail by a registered process server, and include copies of the certified mail receipts addressed to defendants at the premises. Defendants' assertion that they did not receive any copies by certified mail does not rebut the presumption that the process server properly mailed the summons and complaint by certified mail. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2025 line 11. DEFENDANT CHUNLEI LIU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The lease attached to the complaint describes a month-to-month tenancy, which is deemed renewed every month pursuant to Civil Code 1945, and therefore requires written notice of termination in compliance with Civil Code 1946.1. The three-day notice to quit attached to the complaint does not comply with the minimum notice period required by Civil Code 1946.1. This defect in the notice cannot be cured by amending the complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
